title: Browser Issue Cleanup
description: Diagnose browser slowness, popups, bad proxy settings, cache/profile symptoms, and extension risk before applying approved network/browser cleanup.
starter_prompt: Troubleshoot browser issues and only apply approved cleanup or proxy reset actions.

phases.prompt: Scope the browser symptom, preserve browser profiles and saved passwords, and identify whether network/proxy cleanup is relevant.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; function Section($n){ Write-Output ('== ' + $n + ' ==') }; Section 'Installed browsers'; Get-ItemProperty HKLM:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\*,HKLM:\Software\WOW6432Node\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Uninstall\* -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Where-Object { $_.DisplayName -match 'Chrome|Edge|Firefox|Brave|Opera' } | Select-Object DisplayName,DisplayVersion,Publisher,InstallLocation | Sort-Object DisplayName | Format-Table -AutoSize; Section 'Proxy'; netsh winhttp show proxy; Get-ItemProperty 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ProxyEnable,ProxyServer,AutoConfigURL | Format-List; Section 'Browser profile folders'; $paths=@("$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Google\Chrome\User Data", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Edge\User Data", "$env:APPDATA\Mozilla\Firefox\Profiles"); foreach($p in $paths){ if(Test-Path -LiteralPath $p){ [pscustomobject]@{Path=$p;Exists=$true;Items=(Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Measure-Object).Count} } }

phases.prompt: Collect read-only installed-browser, proxy, and profile-folder evidence. Do not modify browser profiles.
phases.completion: Browser and proxy evidence is captured.

phases.prompt: Analyze browser evidence for proxy hijack, profile bloat, extension/manual-review needs, outdated browsers, and next safe checks.
Ask for explicit approval before resetting WinHTTP proxy or flushing browser-related network state. Offer restore point handling if registry/proxy changes are involved.
phases.completion: Approval and restore point decision are recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; Write-Output 'Resetting WinHTTP proxy after approval.'; netsh winhttp reset proxy; Write-Output 'WinHTTP proxy reset complete. Browser profile data was not changed.'

phases.prompt: Run only if the user approved proxy cleanup and evidence supports it. Do not clear browser profiles, passwords, cookies, or history automatically.
phases.completion: Approved proxy cleanup is complete or failure is recorded.

arguments.command: $ErrorActionPreference='Continue'; netsh winhttp show proxy; Get-ItemProperty 'HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Internet Settings' -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue | Select-Object ProxyEnable,ProxyServer,AutoConfigURL | Format-List

phases.prompt: Verify browser-related proxy state after any approved action.
Check that the workflow preserved browser profile data and that repair actions match evidence.

Create a technician and customer-ready handoff report with evidence, findings, actions/tools used, verification status, artifacts/logs, cleanup status, residual risk, and prioritized follow-up next actions.
Keep logs and reports. Remove only workflow-created scratch files.

acceptance_criteria: Read-only browser/proxy evidence is captured before repair.
Browser profile data is not modified automatically.
Proxy cleanup runs only after approval.
Final report includes evidence, actions, cleanup status, risks, and follow-up items.
